input.headline: The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.

raw.headline: The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.

winner: I signed up for the AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to my inbox, but all I get are pictures of my own internal crisis.

candidates.text: I signed up for The World in Pictures, and now I get a daily email that says "You've been featured in our most compelling photographs." Turns out, my cat staring at a banana is more compelling than world events.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with relatable humor and specific example"}

candidates.text: Why did the AP’s ‘World in Pictures’ photographer break up with his camera? It couldn’t *frame* their relationship properly!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'frame' are clever and specific to photography, with a surprise twist that fits the headline well."}

candidates.text: I tried subscribing to "The World in Pictures," but all I got was a box of blank canvases and a note saying, "You see what you want to.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on subscription expectations, concise and specific with a clever play on 'seeing what you want'."}

candidates.text: I asked the news agency to send me the world in pictures, and now my inbox is just full of high-resolution photos of a very confused planet trying to fit into a digital folder.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific metaphor with a touch of absurdity, effectively combining the literal request with a humorous twist."}

candidates.text: I signed up for "The World in Pictures" and now I get a daily email with a photo of a tree, followed by a photo of the same tree, then a photo of someone taking a photo of the tree, and finally a photo of that person's shadow.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on the expectation of compelling photographs."}

candidates.text: I signed up for the AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to my inbox, but all I get are pictures of my own internal crisis.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the expectation of compelling photos, with a specific and relatable punchline."}

candidates.text: I tried to subscribe to "The World in Pictures," but now I'm getting a daily email that just says, "Here's a picture of a cat wearing sunglasses." Maybe AP stands for "Always Photogenic"?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever play on 'AP' standing for 'Always Photogenic.' It ties humor to the headline while maintaining natural English."}

candidates.text: I signed up for *The World in Pictures* because I wanted my inbox to feel like a postcard from every global disaster—you know, just for the aesthetic.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on the headline's promise."}

candidates.text: I asked the photographer for some news photos, but he told me he couldn't send them because he was still working on the "in-box.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'in-box,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity. The setup could be more clearly tied to the headline about receiving photos in an inbox."}
